en.extract:
The red-knobbed imperial pigeon (Ducula rubricera) is a bird species in the family Columbidae. It is found in Bismarck Archipelago and the Solomon Islands archipelago. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. Red-knobbed imperial pigeons frequently forage in groups of five to ten individuals. 
It is classified as a species of least concern by the IUCN.


References

BirdLife International (BLI) (2008): [2008 IUCN Redlist status changes]. Retrieved 2008-MAY-23.

The '''red-knobbed imperial pigeon''' ('''''Ducula rubricera''''') is a [[bird]] [[species]] in the [[family (biology)|family]] [[Columbidae]]. It is found in [[Bismarck Archipelago]] and the [[Solomon Islands (archipelago)|Solomon Islands archipelago]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. Red-knobbed imperial pigeons frequently forage in groups of five to ten individuals.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Callaghan |first1=Corey T. |last2=Kekeubata |first2=Esau |last3=Waneagea |first3=Jackson |last4=Alabai |first4=Maasafi |last5=Esau |first5=Tommy |last6=MacLaren |first6=David |last7=Major |first7=Richard E. |date=2019-12-20 |title=A collaborative bird survey of East Kwaio, Malaita, Solomon Islands |journal=Check List |volume=15 |issue=6 |pages=1119–1136 |doi=10.15560/15.6.1119 |issn=1809-127X |doi-access=free }}</ref> 

It is classified as a species of [[least concern]] by the [[IUCN]].<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021"/>
[[File:Red-knobbed_imperial_pigeon_Zoo_Miami.jpg|thumb|A red-knobbed imperial pigeon at [[Zoo Miami]].]]
